sections.paragraph:
φορέω [φόρος] the idea of bearing or carrying underlies all usage of φορέω: ‘have someth. closely and constantly associated with one’s person’, wear, bear

—a. of putting on clothing routinelywear Mt 11:8; Js 2:3; the idea is extended to wearing of a crown as piece of a costume in the charade described in J 19:5. In imagery 1 Cor 15:49.

—b. of carrying a sword as constant symbol of power and authority carry, bear Ro 13:4.
